title: Money (Jamaican Dollars)
subject: Mathematics
description: Learn to count, add, and subtract Jamaican money

sections.name: Jamaican Coins and Notes
sections.instruction: Jamaica uses dollars ($) and cents (¢). Let's learn about our money!

questions.prompt: Which coin is worth the MOST?
questions.hint: The bigger the number, the more it's worth!

questions.prompt: How many $10 coins make $50?
How many $5 coins make $20?
Which is worth MORE: three $10 coins or two $20 coins?
Select ALL the ways to make $20:
questions.hint: Add up each option to see if it equals $20.

sections.name: Counting Money
sections.instruction: When counting money, start with the biggest coins first!

questions.prompt: Morgan has: $10 + $10 + $5. How much money does she have?
Count the money: $20 + $10 + $5 + $1
Dad has two $20 coins and one $10 coin. How much money does he have?
Morgan saved: $10 + $10 + $10 + $5 + $5. How much did she save?

questions.hint: Add the $20s first, then add the $5s.

sections.name: Adding Money
sections.instruction: When we buy things, we add up the prices to find the total cost.

questions.prompt: A pencil costs $15 and an eraser costs $10. How much for both?
A juice box costs $30 and a bun costs $25. What is the total?
Morgan buys a book for $45 and crayons for $32. How much does she spend?
questions.hint: Add: 45 + 32. Add ones first (5+2=7), then tens (40+30=70).

questions.prompt: A toy car costs $38 and a ball costs $24. What is the total cost?
A snack costs $18 and a drink costs $22. What is the total?

sections.name: Subtracting Money (Change)
sections.instruction: When we pay more than the cost, we get change back!

questions.prompt: Morgan pays $20 for a snack that costs $15. How much change does she get?
Dad pays $50 for groceries that cost $35. What is his change?
A toy costs $42. Morgan pays with $50. How much change?
Mom pays $100 for a dress that costs $75. What change does she receive?
A book costs $28. You pay $30. How much change do you get?

sections.name: Money Word Problems
sections.instruction: Read each problem carefully. Decide if you need to add or subtract!

questions.prompt: Morgan has $35. Her grandmother gives her $20 more. How much does Morgan have now?
questions.hint: She gets MORE money, so we ADD: $35 + $20

questions.prompt: Dad had $80. He spent $45 on food. How much money does he have left?
questions.hint: He SPENT money, so we SUBTRACT: $80 - $45

questions.prompt: Morgan wants to buy a doll for $60. She has saved $42. How much more does she need?
Chris has $25 and Morgan has $30. How much do they have together?
A notebook costs $22 and a pen costs $8. If Morgan has $40, how much will she have left after buying both?
questions.hint: First find the total cost ($22 + $8 = $30), then subtract from $40.
